old_claim.note: Consumer Installment Credit provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Paper Making supplies the document substrate for typed correspondence, forms, filings, and carbon-copy workflows; consumer installment credit is a sales channel rather than an office-workflow prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Overview and History sections: defines carbon paper as sheets used to make copies when inscribed by a typewriter and explains the office term carbon copy.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents paper-based carbon-copy sheets as part of typewriter copying practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected edge is about the paper document substrate for copies and forms, not the financing or sale of typewriters to consumers.

ontology_before: Typewritten Office Workflows depended on Consumer Installment Credit, implying typewriter financing enabled office workflow practice.
ontology_after: Typewritten Office Workflows depends on Paper Making for document substrate and Typewriter for the required office device.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: typewritten_office_workflows now depends on paper_making and no longer directly depends on consumer_installment_credit.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to consumer financing of typewriters rather than office document workflows.
A source shows installment credit was necessary for typed office workflows.
paper_making is removed without a narrower document-substrate or carbon-copy prerequisite.
short_reason: Office workflows need paper documents; installment credit is only sales context.
